audit_status.source_base: Greek Gospel text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for feeding four thousand, sign demand, leaven warning, loaves misunderstanding, Bethsaida two-stage sight, Peter confession, first passion prediction, Peter rebuke, cross-following, psyche language.

verses.restored: In those days, when again there was a great crowd and they had nothing to eat, he called the disciples and said to them,
I am moved with compassion for the crowd, because they remain with me three days and have nothing to eat.
If I release them hungry to their houses, they will faint on the way; and some of them have come from far away.
His disciples answered, From where will anyone be able to feed these people with bread here in a deserted place?
He asked them, How many loaves do you have? They said, Seven.
He instructed the crowd to recline on the ground; taking the seven loaves, giving thanks, he broke them and gave them to his disciples to set before them; and they set them before the crowd.
They also had a few little fish; blessing them, he said to set these before them also.
They ate and were filled, and they took up seven baskets of broken pieces remaining.
There were about four thousand. He released them.
Immediately, entering the boat with his disciples, he came into the region of Dalmanutha.
The Pharisees came out and began to argue with him, seeking from him a sign from heaven, testing him.
Sighing deeply in his spirit, he said, Why does this generation seek a sign? Amen, I say to you: no sign will be given to this generation.
Leaving them, he again entered the boat and went away to the other side.
They forgot to take bread, and except for one loaf they had none with them in the boat.
He instructed them, saying, Watch; beware of the leaven of the Pharisees and the leaven of Herod.
They were reasoning with one another because they had no bread.
Knowing this, he said to them, Why are you reasoning because you have no bread? Do you not yet perceive or understand? Have your hearts been hardened?
Having eyes, do you not see? Having ears, do you not hear? And do you not remember?
When I broke the five loaves for the five thousand, how many baskets full of broken pieces did you take up? They said to him, Twelve.
And the seven for the four thousand, how many baskets full of broken pieces did you take up? They said, Seven.
He said to them, Do you not yet understand?
They came to Bethsaida. They brought to him a blind man and pleaded with him to touch him.
Taking the blind man by the hand, he brought him outside the village; spitting on his eyes and placing hands on him, he asked, Do you see anything?
Looking up, he said, I see people, because I see them like trees walking around.
Then again he placed hands on his eyes, and he looked intently and was restored, and he saw everything clearly.
He sent him away to his house, saying, Do not even enter the village.
Jesus and his disciples went out to the villages of Caesarea Philippi. On the way he questioned his disciples, saying, Who do people say I am?
They said to him, John the Baptist; others, Elijah; others, one of the prophets.
He questioned them, But you, who do you say I am? Peter answered him, You are the Messiah.
He strictly warned them that they should tell no one about him.
He began to teach them that it is necessary for the Son of Humanity to suffer many things, be rejected by the elders, chief priests, and scribes, be killed, and after three days rise.
He was speaking the word plainly. Peter took him aside and began to rebuke him.
Turning and seeing his disciples, he rebuked Peter and said, Go behind me, adversary, because you are not thinking the things of the Divine but the things of humans.
Calling the crowd with his disciples, he said to them, If anyone wants to come after me, let that one deny self, take up the cross, and follow me.
For whoever wants to save his life/soul will lose it, but whoever loses his life/soul because of me and the good message will save it.
For what does it profit a person to gain the whole world and forfeit one's life/soul?
For what can a person give in exchange for one's life/soul?

Whoever is ashamed of me and my words in this adulterous and misaligned generation, of that one the Son of Humanity will also be ashamed when he comes in the glory of his Father with the holy messengers.
